who-am-I.title: Who I am
who-am-I.subtitle: A technical profile with big picture vision
who-am-I.text: I am a technical profile with a solid background in electronic and computer engineering, driven from a young age by music, cinema and curiosity about how complex systems work.
about.myJourney.title: Where I come from
about.myJourney.toptext1: Since my adolescence I have been surrounded by computers and technology. Music was the first catalyst: turntables, mixing desks and electronic equipment sparked in me the interest to understand how the devices and systems behind them work.
about.myJourney.toptext2: That technical drive led me to become interested in professional sound systems and large events, especially in how complex structures like line arrays and large scale audio chains work.
about.myJourney.toptext3: My education went through electricity, telecommunications and computer science, which gave me a solid foundation to later specialize as a sound technician for audiovisuals and shows at IRTVE (Madrid).
about.myJourney.toptext4: Over time, that initial curiosity turned into technical training and professional experience. Not as an accumulation of degrees or isolated jobs, but as a way of understanding how real demanding systems are built, operated and sustained.
about.myJourney.studiesBullet1: Ciclo Formativo de Grado Medio en Instalaciones Eléctricas y Automáticas
about.myJourney.studiesBullet2: Ciclo Formativo de Grado Superior en Sistemas de Telecomunicaciones e Informáticos
about.myJourney.studiesBullet3: Ciclo Formativo de Grado Superior en Sonido para Audiovisuales y Espectáculos (IRTVE)
about.myJourney.experienceBullet1: Work in complex technical environments: events, audiovisual shoots, installations
about.myJourney.experienceBullet2: Experience with large scale sound, lighting and video systems
about.myJourney.experienceBullet3: Collaboration with multidisciplinary teams in high pressure environments
about.myJourney.experienceBullet4: Management and maintenance of technical infrastructures (RTVA, Andorra)
about.myJourney.bottomtext: As I progressed professionally, I understood that what interested me the most was not executing a specific part of the system, but understanding it as a whole. Seeing how every technical, human or organizational decision impacts the final result was what started to define my way of thinking.
about.bigPicture.title: The big picture vision as a common thread
about.bigPicture.toptext1: From the beginning, and especially during my professional experience, I have always had a clear obsession: understanding the complete system.
about.bigPicture.toptext2: From the singer's microphone to the spectator's ear.
about.bigPicture.toptext3: From the capture of a signal to its final delivery.
about.bigPicture.toptext4: Not only understanding technical elements, but also:
about.bigPicture.bullet4: the critical points of the system
about.bigPicture.bottomtext: Understanding that complete chain allowed me to design, anticipate and optimize processes, not just execute an isolated task.
about.professionalGrowth.title: Professional experience
about.professionalGrowth.toptext1: I have worked in events, audiovisual shoots and technical installations, often occupying a part of the total workflow.
about.professionalGrowth.toptext2: But even in those contexts, I have always needed to understand the whole flow.
about.professionalGrowth.toptext3: For example, in environments like a television news:
about.professionalGrowth.bullet1: from the editor and the camera that generates the content
about.professionalGrowth.bullet2: through the technical and human systems
about.professionalGrowth.bullet3: to the live broadcast on air
about.professionalGrowth.bottomtext: That systemic approach has always guided my way of working.
about.learnedInMyCareer.title: What I learned working in real systems
about.learnedInMyCareer.bullet1: Problems are almost never where they fail
about.learnedInMyCareer.bullet2: Optimizing one part usually worsens another
about.learnedInMyCareer.bullet3: Systems are dynamic and change over time
about.learnedInMyCareer.bullet6: Most errors are design errors, not execution errors
about.learnedInMyCareer.bullet7: Pressure reveals if a system is well thought out
about.learnedInMyCareer.bullet8: Understanding the complete flow saves more time than rushing
about.identityLoosingRole.title: The point where something stopped fitting
about.identityLoosingRole.toptext1: For years I worked executing specific parts of complex systems.
about.identityLoosingRole.toptext2: Although I learned a lot, over time I understood that I didn't want to keep occupying just an operational role within a previously designed system.
about.identityLoosingRole.toptext3: My motivation has always been to understand, structure and improve the whole, not to put out fires or improvise partial solutions.
about.identityLoosingRole.toptext4: That was the moment I decided to redirect my path towards system design, coding, and infrastructure.
about.transitionToSoftware.title: Where I'm going
about.transitionToSoftware.toptext1: Today I focus my professional development on:
about.transitionToSoftware.bottomtext: I continue to build my path from that same logic: understand before executing, design before scaling, and think of systems as a coherent whole. This approach guides both my learning and my professional evolution.
